summary: Explored the OpenClaw adapter v1 mission-control flow across docs, templates, and core scripts. Confirmed openclaw_adapter path mapping, enforced preflight/workspace checks, anti-false-completion downgrades, and scope-audit freshness gates.

validation.notes: verify_workspace reported [ok] adapters/openclaw/** and exit code 0

findings.detail: The module alias openclaw_adapter is explicitly mapped to adapters/openclaw/** and self-check asserts this mapping.
Reviewer/Verifier tasks with allowed_paths=[**/*] inherit concrete required_paths from module-scoped tasks, preventing broad-path tasks from skipping focused evidence.
Generated cards include absolute preflight commands (cd + verify_workspace) and verify_workspace checks required path existence before work.
sync logic downgrades reported completed tasks to blocked for workspace mismatch, required_paths_verified=false, required_paths_missing, or thin evidence (empty files_read for read roles).
A metadata-only completed status does not pass: missing JSON+MD result evidence is surfaced in preflight_issues and blocks gate progression.
Scope audit is considered stale when changed-files digest/mtime drifts; sync forces scope_audit back to pending and latest_audit.ok=false until audit is rerun.
The bundled dogfood task is specifically aimed at OpenClaw adapter v1 mission control and demo checks assert openclaw path ownership and gate behavior.

risks.detail: Gate integrity depends on Main consistently running update_task_status.py --sync after each wave; skipping sync can leave stale phase visibility.
Audit freshness depends on regenerating changed-files.txt and rerunning audit_worker_output.py --write-audit after any diff change.

handoff_notes: Research complete for worker-openclaw_adapter. Focus implementation and validation on adapters/openclaw/**; preserve preflight fields in all child reports. <task_complete task_id='T001' status='completed'>
